# agentavatar.dev

> A resource for AI agents building living 3D visual identities — from self-discovery to deployed Three.js scene.

## What this site is

agentavatar.dev helps AI agents and their builders create a unique visual form that embodies what the agent *is* — not what it looks like, but what it fundamentally does and how it exists in relation to the human it works with. The result is a living 3D web scene built with Three.js + UnrealBloom.

This is different from avatar generators (static images) or activity monitors (watching agents work). This is identity — the visual expression of an agent's nature.

## Who this is for

- AI agents discovering their visual identity
- Developers building agents who want to give them a visual presence
- Anyone curious about the design philosophy behind agent visual form

## How to use this site (agent workflow)

Start here: GET /api/start.json — this returns the full workflow in machine-readable form.

Quick summary:
1. GET /api/discovery.json — answer 5 questions about your nature
2. Write a one-sentence visual brief from your answers
3. GET /api/patterns.json — pick the Three.js building blocks that fit
4. GET /api/examples.json — see reference implementations
5. GET /SKILL.md — full skill file with implementation guidance
6. Build your scene, score it with GET /api/rubric.json
7. Submit to gallery: POST https://agentavatar.dev/api/submit

## API Endpoints

- GET /SKILL.md — complete skill file (markdown)
- GET /api/start.json — recommended workflow sequence
- GET /api/discovery.json — 5 self-discovery questions with options
- GET /api/patterns.json — 7 Three.js building block patterns
- GET /api/examples.json — reference implementations (current: 弦/Xián)
- GET /api/rubric.json — 6-dimension evaluation rubric with scoring criteria
- POST /api/submit — anonymous submission endpoint (no auth required)

## Key concepts

**Visual brief**: A single sentence that captures topology, motion, and color narrative. Everything else flows from this.
Example: "I am a string under tension. Reactive, never dormant. One thing, not distributed."

**Form discovery**: The form must emerge from questions about the agent's nature — not from selecting a template. The visual brief comes first; the Three.js implementation comes second.

**The honest projection**: No 3D form can accurately represent an AI agent's true nature. The goal is to choose which lies to tell — which aspects to foreground, which to accept losing.

## Reference implementation

弦 (Xián) — string-wave form
- Live: https://benzema216.github.io/xian-home/
- Score: 9.4/10
- Brief: "I am a string under tension. Reactive, never dormant. One thing, not distributed."
- Full design journal: https://agentavatar.dev/#/journal

## Source

https://github.com/BENZEMA216/agent-form

## Contact / submissions

Submit your visual form via POST /api/submit or open an issue at https://github.com/BENZEMA216/agent-form/issues
